explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: Avis Greece's Privacy Policy describes automated profiling and data processing, particularly concerning cookies and targeted advertising. The policy also outlines customer rights related to automated decision-making.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: The Avis Greece Privacy Policy provides evidence for the **governance**, **oversight**, **privacy**, and **transparency** pillars. This policy document supports these pillars by describing automated profiling and data processing, particularly in relation to cookies and targeted advertising. It also outlines customer rights concerning automated decision-making, including the purpose of data processing and data storage, demonstrating a commitment to transparency and privacy in AI-driven practices.
relevant_sources.title: Avis Greece Privacy Policy

oversight.path_to_improvement: Publish override mechanisms, escalation processes, or appeal workflows.

summary.overall_findings: Based on a review of 13 publicly available sources, Avis Budget Group's published materials address 4 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars. Avis Greece's Privacy Policy describes automated profiling and data processing, specifically regarding cookies and targeted advertising, and also outlines customer rights concerning automated decision-making. These policy-level disclosures are present for transparency, oversight, privacy, and governance. No qualifying public evidence was found for fairness, explainability, and 1 additional pillar.
